analysis_result: Macrophages represent 4189/7184 = 58.3% of immune cells (PTPRC+ Leiden clusters) in MPNST-G2 snRNA-seq (M1933 + M1677). Macrophage clusters identified: ['3', '10', '11'].
denominator_used: N = 7184 immune cells, defined as cells in PTPRC+ Leiden clusters ['3', '4', '6', '7', '9', '10', '11', '12'] (mean PTPRC log-norm > 1.0 or >30% PTPRC+ cells), across MPNST-G2 samples M1933 and M1677 after QC (>=500 genes, <10% mito).
assumptions_made: MPNST-G2 = M1933 + M1677 (per paper sample grouping); QC thresholds: >=500 genes, <10% mito (MT- prefix, human Ensembl features); HVG via seurat_v3 (n=3000) with batch_key='sample'; 50 PCs, top 30 used for kNN (n_neighbors=15); Leiden clustering at resolution 0.8, random_state=42; Immune cluster criterion: mean PTPRC log-norm > 1.0 OR fraction PTPRC+ cells > 30%; Macrophage cluster criterion (data-driven for snRNA-seq): macrophage score (mean of CD14, CD68, CD163, AIF1, MARCO, MRC1 log-norm) above median+MAD of immune clusters, AND >=2 of {CD14, CD163, MRC1, CD68} with mean log-norm > 0.3, AND >=2 of those markers with pct>0 > 15%. Cluster-level (not per-cell) macrophage assignment; nuclear RNA capture lowers surface-marker detection.
